Pavement Distress Diagnostic

Alaska Context — Select an observed distress to trace causes ← Field Guide Home
Step 1
What do you see?
```
〰️
Longitudinal cracking
Cracks parallel to centerline, at wheel path or shoulder edge
━━
Transverse cracking
Regular cracks perpendicular to centerline, evenly spaced
⬡
Alligator cracking
Interconnected fatigue cracks in wheel path, mesh pattern
⌇
Rutting
Longitudinal depression in wheel paths, permanent deformation
○
Potholes
Bowl-shaped holes through the surface, often water-filled
shared
◫
Delamination
Surface layer separating from layer below, chunks peeling free
△
Frost heave / irregular cracking
Upward displacement, irregular cracking correlated to soil type
shared
∴
Raveling
Loss of aggregate from surface, rough texture, coarsening
⇉
Shoving / corrugation
Rippled or pushed surface, crescent-shaped displacement
```
↖

Select a distress type
to trace its causes

Step 3
Field notes

to see field notes,
investigation approach,
and Alaska context.
